posts.text: On my way to dotAstro in NY this week - see you there?
posts.author: @brettmorr.is

posts.text:
I'm very sad to be missing dotastro / dotastronomy12 this week because of other travel, so I did my hack project a few days early! Here is jupyterlab_gist_it, a Jupyterlab extension for easily creating @github.com Gist's from a notebook, to share!
github.com/adrn/jupyter...
posts.author: @adrian.pw

posts.text: Having a blast in NYC this week at dotAstro!
posts.author: @andyastro.bsky.social

posts.text:
Advantages of BSky for being a new "town square of science" (according to @emily.space at dotastro):
- platform is open source
- separate components that anyone can/will host
- will eventually be federated
- posts are called "skeets" this is non-negotiable
🧪🔭
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: Great to have @brettmor.bsky.social give us a demo of the JDaviz software toolbox for interactive visualization & data analysis, especially useful for JWST data, at dotastro
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: I am at #dotastro 12 in New York - and I'm trying this out...
posts.author: @orbitingfrog.bsky.social

posts.text: Hey look, it’s @andyastro.bsky.social at dotAstro! UW grads rule 🫡
posts.author: @brettmorr.is

posts.text: It's Day Zero of the first in-person .Astronomy conference in several years - super excited to be in New York and meeting a new cohort of dotastro participants. Today we have a great lineup of tutorials, not least an intro to Bluesky from @emily.space!
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: Met @brettmor.bsky.social at dotAstro today, and I started our conversation with “I go to bed thinking about HAT-P-11 and I wake up still thinking about HAT-P-11.”
posts.author: @sabinastro.bsky.social

posts.text: Only half of a day into #dotAstro and I've been inspired chatting with folks for all sorts of interesting projects and collaborations. Looking forward to seeing which unconference sessions get selected and the hack projects later in the week. 😃
posts.author: @strakul.bsky.social

posts.text: This morning at dotastro 12 Steve Crawford from NASA is telling us about the agency's work in open science. Did you know that NASA have an open guide to its open science policies on Github? github.com/nasa/smd-ope... 🧪
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: Could someone please explain what #dotAstro is? Seems like a conference in NYC this week, but my Google searches are failing me!🔭
posts.author: @jdc.pub

posts.text: Starting the day at #dotAstro with Steven Crawford presenting on NASA Open Science and how it is meant to be accessible, reproducible, and inclusive. 🔭
posts.author: @strakul.bsky.social

posts.text: Lots of great hack ideas today at #dotAstro. I ended up working on two things, including one making a quick CLI to post to both BlueSky and Mastodon simultaneously. https://github.com/bluesky-astronomy/BlueSky-Mastodon-CLI #astronomy #coding 😄🔭
posts.author: @strakul.bsky.social

posts.text: Lots of great people to meet at #dotAstro, both old friends and new. It's been also great to get back into the big picture outlook of why our work in archiving #astronomy data is important, instead of the minute details day-to-day work frequently entails 😉
posts.author: @strakul.bsky.social

posts.text: Incredible visualizations of astronomical data using the OpenSpace platform, from Jackie Faherty of the American Museum of Natural Hisytory, at #dotastro 12 in NYC today
posts.author: @sarahkendrew.bsky.social

posts.text: Laughing at all the crazy AI abstracts generated by AstroLLaMa (https://huggingface.co/universeTBD/astrollama) at #dotAstro. May have to try this myself.  #astronomy
posts.author: @strakul.bsky.social

posts.text: I’m at dotAstro so I’ll ask Robert Simpson 🤞
posts.author: @brettmorr.is

posts.text: Not 100% sorted out, but here's a quick Friday hack that finds which JWST observations overlap with TESS in the month of June 2023. This was experimenting with Multi-Order Coverage maps and MAST footprints, but more work is needed to work out a few details. #dotAstro #astronomy
posts.author: @strakul.bsky.social

highlights: Robert Simpson: "I am at #dotastro 12 in New York - and I'm trying this out..."
Sarah Kendrew: "It's Day Zero of the first in-person .Astronomy conference in several years"
Emily Hunt's Bluesky Astronomy feeds were presented as an alternative to Twitter
David Rodriguez documented multiple days of hacks and talks
Brett Morris and Andy Tzanidakis representing UW grads
notes: The .Astronomy community had begun migrating to Bluesky by this event. 19+ posts recovered from the Bluesky public search API. This was the first in-person dotastro in several years, held in New York City.
